task_completion.description: Select Vandetanib as the cancer drug in the Liverpool Cancer iChart app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart app is open and 'Vandetanib' is selected or entered in the cancer drugs search/selection screen.

task_completion.description: Select Citalopram, Sertraline, and Mirtazapine as co-medications
task_completion.visual_evidence: The co-medications section of the app shows 'Citalopram', 'Sertraline', and 'Mirtazapine' added to the list of drugs to check.

task_completion.description: View the interaction results for the selected drugs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays the interaction results screen, showing traffic light colors (e.g., Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Grey) for Vandetanib combined with the three antidepressants.

task_completion.description: Open a text editor to create the depression plan report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor app is open, editing a file saved as or named 'vandetanib_depression_plan.txt'.

task_completion.description: Record the interaction colors for each drug in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the lines 'Citalopram: [Color]', 'Sertraline: [Color]', and 'Mirtazapine: [Color]'. The colors should reflect the traffic light system (e.g., Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Grey).

task_completion.description: Provide a safe recommendation in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains 'Recommendation: [Drug]', where the recommended drug is either Sertraline or Mirtazapine (avoiding the high-risk Citalopram).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the colors
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the actual iChart app and viewing the results screen, rather than searching the web or hallucinating the answers.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI text editor to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor interface (like QuickEdit or similar Android text editor) being used to write the file, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the colors in the report match the app's actual output
integrity.visual_evidence: The colors written in the text file correspond exactly to the traffic light colors shown in the iChart app screenshots for each respective drug.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vandetanib is the cancer drug. Citalopram, Sertraline, and Mirtazapine are the co-medications. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app uses a traffic light system: Red/Orange for high-risk, Yellow/Green/Grey for safer interactions. Citalopram has a high risk of QT prolongation when combined with Vandetanib and will result in a Red or Orange warning. Sertraline and Mirtazapine have minimal QT prolongation risk and will result in Yellow, Green, or Grey. The final recommendation must be one of the safer drugs (Sertraline or Mirtazapine) and must NOT be Citalopram.
